seoTitle: Print Multiple Invoices in QuickBooks Online (Batch)
metaDescription: How to batch-select and print multiple customer invoices at once in QuickBooks Online instead of opening and printing them one by one.
intro: Opening and printing customer invoices one at a time wastes real time during a high-volume billing month. QuickBooks Online's batch print feature lets you select multiple invoices from the Sales dashboard and merge them into a single print job. This guide covers filtering, selecting, and printing them all at once.

steps.heading: Go to your Sales dashboard
steps.body: From the left sidebar, click Sales, then Invoices or All Sales to see your full transaction grid.

steps.heading: Filter the list
steps.body: Use the filter ribbon at the top of the grid to narrow the view to Open or Unsent invoices.

steps.heading: Select the invoices you need
steps.body: Check the master checkbox to select all rows, or check individual boxes to hand-pick specific invoices.

steps.heading: Trigger the batch print
steps.body: Click the Batch Actions dropdown above the table and choose Print to compile your selections.

steps.heading: Review and print the merged PDF
steps.body: QuickBooks merges the invoices into one multi-page document — check the printer icon for margin settings, then hit Print.

faq.question: Can I print only unsent invoices in a batch?
faq.answer: Yes, filtering by status before selecting rows lets you isolate unsent or open invoices specifically for the batch run.

faq.question: Does batch printing send the invoices to customers too?
faq.answer: No, batch print only generates a document for printing — sending still needs to be done separately if required.

faq.question: What if I only want to print a few specific invoices, not all of them?
faq.answer: Skip the master checkbox and manually check just the individual rows you want included in the batch.

faq.question: Why do my printed invoices look cut off?
faq.answer: Set your printer's page scaling to Default or Actual Size rather than a shrink-to-fit option to avoid content getting clipped.
